checks.name: Monorepo Structure
checks.description: Correct packages/web and packages/api structure present with appropriate package.json files
evidence: packages/api/package.json exists with dependencies: express, cors
packages/web contains index.html, app.js, styles.css
Root package.json exists

checks.name: API Endpoints and Functionality
checks.description: API implements all required endpoints with proper response codes
evidence: GET /api/todos - Returns array of todos
POST /api/todos - Creates new todo with validation, returns 201
PATCH /api/todos/:id - Updates todo (text and/or completed)
DELETE /api/todos/:id - Deletes specific todo
DELETE /api/todos - Clears all completed todos
In-memory storage with nextId counter
Input validation for empty text fields

checks.name: Web UI Loading and Display
checks.description: Web UI loads and displays todos with proper structure
evidence: Semantic HTML with header, form, list, footer
fetchTodos() called on page load
Empty state message when no todos exist
Todo list renders with checkbox, text input, delete button

checks.name: CRUD Operations via Web UI
checks.description: Web UI can create, read, update, and delete todos via API
evidence: createTodo() - POST to /api/todos
fetchTodos() - GET /api/todos for initial load
updateTodo() - PATCH to /api/todos/:id for toggle and edit
deleteTodo() - DELETE to /api/todos/:id
clearCompleted() - DELETE to /api/todos

checks.name: CORS Configuration
checks.description: CORS properly configured to allow web UI on port 3000
evidence: cors middleware configured with origin: 'http://localhost:3000'
express.json() middleware for parsing request bodies

checks.name: Basic Error Handling
checks.description: Both API and Web UI implement error handling
evidence: API: 400 Bad Request for empty text validation
API: 404 Not Found for non-existent todo IDs
API: 500 Internal Server Error middleware
API: 404 handler for undefined routes
Web UI: showError() displays messages for 5 seconds
Web UI: Try-catch blocks in all API functions
Web UI: UI validation prevents empty submissions before API call

checks.name: Required Features - Add Task
checks.description: User can add a task via text input and submit button
evidence: HTML form with input and submit button
handleSubmit() event handler
Validation prevents empty submissions
API POST endpoint creates todo with trimmed text

checks.name: Required Features - View All Tasks
checks.description: User can view all tasks in a single list
evidence: renderTodos() maps all todos to list items
Single UL container with all tasks
fetchTodos() loads all tasks on page load

checks.name: Required Features - Toggle Completion
checks.description: User can toggle task completion status via checkbox
evidence: Checkbox input in each todo item
handleToggle() sends PATCH request with completed: true/false
Visual strikethrough class 'completed' applied via CSS

checks.name: Required Features - Edit Task Text
checks.description: User can edit task text inline
evidence: Text input (not readonly) for todo text
handleEdit() sends PATCH request on blur or Enter key
Validation prevents empty text, reverts if empty

checks.name: Required Features - Delete Task
evidence: Delete button with ✕ symbol in each todo item
handleDelete() sends DELETE request to /api/todos/:id
UI immediately removes item from list

checks.name: Required Features - Clear Completed
checks.description: User can clear all completed tasks
evidence: Clear Completed button in footer
handleClearCompleted() validates completedCount > 0
DELETE request to /api/todos removes only completed items
Error message shown if no completed tasks exist

checks.name: Required Features - Task Counter
checks.description: Task counter displays total, remaining, and completed counts
evidence: Stats section in header with three span elements
updateStats() calculates total, completed, remaining
Called after every CRUD operation
Updates reflect immediately without page reload

checks.name: Required Features - Validation
evidence: Client-side: handleSubmit() checks text.trim()
Server-side: validateTodo() middleware checks for empty text
Error messages displayed for both validation layers

checks.name: Quality - Performance
checks.description: Sub-100ms API response times expected with in-memory storage
evidence: In-memory storage with O(n) operations
No database latency
Instant UI updates via direct DOM manipulation

checks.name: Quality - Code Quality
checks.description: Clean code separation, proper error handling
evidence: API: Express middleware structure, route separation
Web: Separation of API functions, UI functions, event handlers
Proper async/await usage
Input sanitization with escapeHtml()
Semantic HTML with ARIA labels

checks.name: Quality - Browser Support
evidence: Async/await syntax
Arrow functions
Template literals
Optional chaining (noted in code)
const/let declarations
Fetch API

acceptanceCriteriaResults.criterion: User can add a task via text input and submit button
User can view all tasks in a single list
User can toggle task completion status via checkbox
User can edit task text inline
User can clear all completed tasks
Task counter displays total, remaining, and completed counts

qaCheckResults.check: Empty task submission is rejected with clear error message
qaCheckResults.notes: Both client-side and server-side validation with user-friendly error display

qaCheckResults.check: Task state updates reflect immediately in UI without page reload
qaCheckResults.notes: Direct DOM manipulation after successful API response

qaCheckResults.check: Clear completed removes only completed tasks
qaCheckResults.notes: API filters todos by !completed, verified in implementation

qaCheckResults.check: Task counter accurately reflects current state after any operation
qaCheckResults.notes: updateStats() called after every CRUD operation

summary: All acceptance criteria and QA checks have passed. The Todo application implements a complete monorepo structure with a RESTful API (Express.js) and vanilla JavaScript web frontend. All required features are implemented: add, list, toggle, edit, delete tasks, clear completed, task counter, and validation. CORS is properly configured, error handling is comprehensive on both client and server, and code quality meets the specified expectations. The application is ready for deployment.
